data.title: Electrolux Q4 operating profit, sales rise
data.description:
Overview

  • Sweden appliance maker's Q4 organic sales grew 2% driven by higher volumes

  • Operating income improved significantly due to cost efficiency

  • Company, on Jan 30, announced organizational changes to enhance consumer focus

Outlook

  • Electrolux expects North American market demand to be neutral to negative in 2026

  • Company anticipates SEK 3.5-4bn earnings contribution from cost efficiency in 2026

  • Electrolux projects increased capital expenditure compared to 2025

Result Drivers

  • COST EFFICIENCY - Electrolux achieved SEK 1.2bn in cost savings, improving operating income despite external challenges

  • VOLUME GROWTH - Higher sales volumes in focus product categories supported organic sales growth in most regions

  • PRICING PRESSURE - North America faced pricing pressure, leading to sales decline despite previous price increases

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

Q4 Revenue

SEK 35.11 bln

Q4 EPS

SEK 1.72

Q4 Net Income

SEK 466 mln

Q4 EBIT

SEK 1.52 bln

Q4 Organic Growth

2.00%

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 4 "strong buy" or "buy", 7 "hold" and 2 "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the appliances, tools & housewares peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Electrolux AB is SEK67.50, about 7.8% above its January 29 closing price of SEK62.64

  • The stock recently traded at 7 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 7 three months ago

Press Release:

data.title: WDP: Strong earnings growth, high occupancy, and upgraded credit rating drive robust 2025 results
data.description:
EPRA Earnings per share grew 7% y/y to €1.53, with strong leasing and a 97.7% occupancy rate. Moody's upgraded the credit rating to A3, and a €500 million Green Bond was issued. 2026 guidance targets 5% EPS growth and a €1.29 dividend per share.

Original document: Warehouses De Pauw SA [WDP] Earnings Release — Jan. 30 2026

data.title: Norway's Scatec Q4 revenue rises 25% on project progress
data.description:
Overview

  • Norwegian renewable energy firm's Q4 proportionate revenue rose 25% yr/yr

  • Q4 EBITDA was NOK 1,065 million, driven by construction progress in Egypt and South Africa

  • Company reached several milestones, including new solar projects in Philippines and Africa

Outlook

  • Scatec projects full-year 2026 power production of 5.2 to 5.6 TWh

  • Company expects full-year 2026 EBITDA from power production of NOK 3,800 to 4,100 mln

  • Remaining D&C contract value for projects under construction is NOK 1.8 bln

Result Drivers

  • CONSTRUCTION PROGRESS - Strong construction progress on Obelisk in Egypt and Mogobe BESS in South Africa drove D&C segment revenues

  • DIVESTMENTS IMPACT - Decline in power production revenues attributed to divestments in 2024 and 2025

  • FINANCIAL IMPROVEMENTS - Reduced net interest bearing debt and strong cash generation improved financial position

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

NOK 3.36 bln

  • The average consensus recommendation for the independent power producers peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Scatec ASA is NOK129.00, about 7.1% above its January 29 closing price of NOK120.40

  • The stock recently traded at 26 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 18 three months ago

data.title: Maisons du Monde Q4 revenue misses estimates as online sales fall
data.description:
Overview

  • French home retailer's Q4 sales fell 5.9%, missing analyst expectations

  • Company achieved €45 mln cost savings in 2025, plans €30 mln more in 2026

  • Online sales dropped 10% in Q4, company to enhance digital experience in 2026

Outlook

  • Company withholds €100 mln cumulative FCF objective for 2024-2027 due to macroeconomic uncertainty

  • Maisons du Monde targets €30 mln cost reduction in 2026 focusing on logistics and HQ optimization

  • Company aims to enhance digital customer journey throughout 2026

Result Drivers

  • ONLINE SALES DECLINE - Online sales fell 10% in Q4, attributed to underperformance in France and Northern countries

  • COST SAVINGS - Achieved €45 mln cost savings in 2025, with a new target of €30 mln for 2026

  • CHRISTMAS COLLECTION - Christmas collection saw a 15% increase in sales, reinforcing Maisons du Monde's position in home decoration

Key Details

data.title: ZAWYA-PRESSR: Dubai Media launches Dubai+, a new digital platform offering safe, family-friendly content
data.description:
First published: 30-Jan-2026 10:05:22

Mona Al Marri: Dubai Media Council’s strategic role and ongoing efforts are focused on strengthening Dubai’s media ecosystem and enhancing the sector’s competitiveness

Mohamed AlMulla: Dubai+ embodies Dubai Media’s strategy to build a fully integrated digital media ecosystem

Dubai+ redefines digital content in Dubai and the Middle East, offering a rich library of local, Arab, and international series and films

At launch, the platform will feature more than 20 exclusive series, six original productions, and over 170 international movies

Government of Dubai Media Office: Under the directives of His Highness Sheikh Ahmed bin Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, Second Deputy Ruler of Dubai and Chairman of the Dubai Media Council (DMC), Dubai Media Incorporated (DMI) launched its new digital platform Dubai+. The launch marks a strategic step in advancing Dubai Media’s digital transformation and reinforcing its position as a leading regional and global hub for content creation.

The announcement was made during a press conference held today, attended by Her Excellency Mona Ghanem Al Marri, Vice Chairperson and Managing Director of the Dubai Media Council and Director General of the Government of Dubai Media Office; His Excellency Mohamed AlMulla, Chief Executive Officer of Dubai Media Incorporated; alongside senior media leaders, representatives of local and regional media, and industry influencers.

Her Excellency Mona Ghanem Al Marri affirmed that the Dubai Media Council’s strategic role and ongoing efforts are focused on strengthening Dubai’s media ecosystem and enhancing the sector’s competitiveness. “Dubai+ reflects the emirate’s ambitious vision to keep pace with global shifts in media and digital broadcasting. We seek to enhance Dubai’s competitiveness as a regional and global hub for creativity and media production by strengthening the industry ecosystem. These efforts include encouraging greater private-sector participation and building advanced storytelling capabilities aligned with Dubai’s future vision, along with raising operational performance, driving innovation, expanding audience impact, investing in talent and skills development, and leveraging technology and advanced production capabilities to catalyse the sector’s growth,” she said.

“What we are witnessing today is the extension of a long-standing approach to building a leading media ecosystem over more than five decades, one that leads digital innovation through a forward-looking government vision that ensures sustained impact,” Her Excellency added.

Comprehensive and diverse platform

His Excellency Mohamed AlMulla, Chief Executive Officer of Dubai Media Incorporated, said the launch of Dubai+ embodies Dubai Media’s strategy to build a fully integrated digital media ecosystem that strengthens its global competitiveness while delivering high-quality, diverse, and innovative content.

He explained that the launch marks an important milestone in the platform’s development, with future phases set to expand its content offering and deepen audience engagement – further positioning Dubai+ as an integrated digital destination for Arab audiences.

His Excellency noted that the platform is the result of an intensive technical and creative development journey. DMI’s teams built advanced digital infrastructure and conducted rigorous testing to ensure high performance and a user experience on par with leading global platforms, fully equipped to support a rich and dynamic slate of programming and sports content.

Extensive library of premieres and exclusives

Salem Balyouha, Chief Executive Officer of Media Content at Dubai Media Incorporated, said: “With the launch of Dubai+, we are entering a new phase in the evolution of Dubai Media’s digital model, bringing a significant addition to the region’s digital broadcasting and video-on-demand landscape.

He added: “We are proud to introduce a platform that places family safety at the heart of the viewing experience. This commitment is reflected not only in the nature of the content we offer, but also in clear selection standards, robust classification systems, and transparent viewing controls that enable audiences to choose with confidence and give families genuine peace of mind.”

Balyouha also explained that Dubai+ features an impressive library of first-run and exclusive film and series releases, original productions, live television channels, sporting competitions, and major events hosted in Dubai. At launch, the platform will feature more than 20 exclusive series, six original productions, over 170 international titles, and a dedicated sports catalogue covering 12 live championships.

Dubai+ is set to redefine digital content in Dubai and the wider Middle East by strengthening the presence of Emirati and Arab content and delivering it to the highest production standards, reflecting a commitment to innovation, evolution, and relevance.

The platform also provides an empowering digital environment for nurturing regional media talent and creatives, supporting local production, launching new initiatives, and expanding partnerships across multiple markets to help Arab content reach wider global audiences.

The launch of Dubai+ underscores DMI’s leadership in shaping the media landscape and reinforces Dubai’s position as a leading regional and global hub for the content industry.

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Lights maker Signify reports full-year results below market expectations
data.description:
Signify slightly missed market forecasts for full-year sales and core earnings on Friday, underscoring the deep challenges the world's largest lights maker faces from weak U.S. demand and a slowdown in its key professional segment.

The company reported full-year sales of 5.77 billion euros ($6.88 billion), compared with analysts' average estimate of 5.81 billion euros in a poll compiled by Signify.

Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes and amortisation (EBITA) were 511 million euros in 2025, against a consensus forecast of 541 million euros.

($1 = 0.8391 euros)

data.title: Aluminum Corp. of China's Brazil Deal Seems Attractively Priced — Market Talk
data.description: Aluminum Corp. of China's proposal to buy a stake in a Brazilian aluminum producer through a joint venture with Rio Tinto seems attractively priced, say Citi analysts in a note. The joint venture--which Chalco will have a 67% stake in--is planning to buy around 69% of Companhia Brasileira de Aluminio for 4.69 billion reais. The proposed price implies 13X 2025 price-to-earnings ratio based on the annualized net profit of the target company, the analysts say. This is attractive given likely stronger aluminum prices in 2026. The proposed buy could expand Chalco's aluminum capacity by 6%, they add. Citi maintains its buy rating and HK$15.94 target price on Chalco, which is down 7.05% at HK$13.97. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)
